text: Ama, bu be mmɔɔ abɔ má bu nu. Na ba ka bu juma n yo su. Kun kɔɔri i fiesu wɔ, na nyiŋga gusu akɔ i juma yoo­biri.
translated: They say that when you are going to the farm, you should not talk. And when you are going to the forest, you should not whistle. If you whistle while you are going to the farm, the birds will eat the crops, and if you talk while you are going to the forest, the animals will run away.

translated: He saw James the son of Zebedee and John his brother, who were in their boat mending the nets, and he called them.

ref: From the time of John the Baptist until now, violent people have been trying to take over the kingdom of heaven by force.
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, “Fite saŋga bo Yohane Nyɛmɛ Nzue Biɛ­fɔ n boori Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ kan n bu ni, fa ba ju ni kisa, ye Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ŋuuri kekereke a, na minɛm afa anyum­boro ka bu wura nu de i.
translated: And Jesus began to say, "What did you go out into the wilderness to see, a reed shaken by the wind, but what did you go out to see, a man clothed in soft garments, behold those who wear soft garments are in kings' houses, but what did you go out to see, a prophet, yes, I say to you, and more than a prophet."

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i sere Nyɛmɛ deke be, i má i, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i niɛniɛ deke be, i ŋú i, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i bobo anɔ ni, i téke i ma i.
translated: The woman's husband went to the river to catch fish, he caught some, and the husband went to the bush to catch game, he caught some, and the husband went to the farm to work, he worked and came back home.

ref: In the towns where Jesus had worked most of his miracles, the people refused to turn to God. So Jesus was upset with them and said:

translated: They were washing their nets on the shore, when Jesus saw Simon's boat on the lake, he got in and sat down.

ref: where they stayed until Herod died. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I called my son out of Egypt.” The Killing of the Children
text: na ba wo dɔ haari Fɛmɛ Herode aba awu. Ahin yoori sɔ ni, i ma kabo Micɛra n ma i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n yo jɔjɔɔri n yo yii­wa a. I ŋa, “Ma fere m wa n afite Ijipiti asiɛn n su.” Kabo Fɛmɛ Herode ma bu kuunri nbaa­biɛsɔ kan kanm ni
translated: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem in Judea, during the time of King Herod, Magi from the east came to Jerusalem and asked, "Where is the one who has been born king of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and have come to worship him." When King Herod heard this he was disturbed, and all Jerusalem with him.

ref: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?
text: Ambɛrɛ nu, ŋma ye i wa sere i diire, ye i mi kéte na i fá yabuɛ ma i wa n a? Ai, be yo má sɔ.
translated: They said, "The child that you gave birth to and you are nursing, what is it that you will do with it when you die?" Ah, they didn't know.

ref: The servants came and asked, “Sir, didn't you scatter good seed in your field? Where did these weeds come from?”
text: Na awuru­fɔ­biɛ ni akɔɔm aba i dɔ, aba ase i ka, ‘Ya mibiɛ, m ŋa ya sandiiri nvoni kpa fie n nu e. Ye jaŋmɔŋgu nvoni n fiteeri ni, kɔ saŋgaari nu a?’
translated: The rabbit said to the monkey, 'My friend, I am going to the sandy forest to dig a hole to sleep in. The scorpion's hole is too narrow for me, where will I sleep?'

ref: But Peter said, “Even if I have to die with you, I will never say I don't know you.” All the others said the same thing. Jesus Prays (Mark

translated: Peter answered him, "Lord, if it's you, tell me to come to you on the water." Then he said, "Come." And Peter got out of the boat and walked on the water and came to Jesus.
